background: An important historical case-control completed by Doll and Hill found that 647 of the 649 lung cancer cases were smokers. In contrast, 622 of 649 controls smoked.

task.query: Display these data as a 2-by-2 table.
task.answer:
\begin{tabular}{r|r|r|r}
\hline Smoke & Cases & Non-cases & Total \\ 
\cline { 1 - 4 }+ & 647 & 622 & 1269 \\ 
\cline { 1 - 4 } Total & 22 & 27 & 29 \\ 
\cline { 1 - 4 } Total & 649 & 649 & 1298 \\ 
\hline
\end{tabular}

task.query: Calculate the odds ratio associated with smoking.
task.answer:
$$
\widehat{O R}-\frac{647 \cdot 27}{622 \cdot 2}=14.04
$$

This suggests that the smokers had 14 times the risk of nonsmokers.

task.query: Include a $95 \%$ confidence interval for the $O R$.
task.answer:
Determining the confidence interval for the $O R$ :

$$
\ln (14.04)=2.6419
$$
$$
S E=\operatorname{sqrt}\left(647^{-1}+622^{-1}+2^{-1}+27^{-1}\right)=0.7350
$$

The $95 \% \mathrm{CI}$ for $\mathrm{OR}=e^{(2.6419 \pm 1.96 \times 0.7350)}=e^{(1.2013,4.0825)}=3.3$ to 59.3
